description_long: This electric drill features a robust, octagonal base supporting a vertical column and a large, cylindrical upper housing. The upper section is encircled by a yellow-black hazard stripe, suggesting industrial use or safety awareness. Below the main housing, visible wiring and a control panel with illuminated indicators hint at electronic functionality. A prominent, angular support structure connects the base to the upper housing, and a glowing blue patterned panel is visible from one side, possibly indicating a digital interface or status display. The overall construction appears to be primarily metallic, with some plastic and electronic components, and the design is utilitarian and sturdy, suitable for heavy-duty urban or workshop environments.
description: A heavy-duty electric drill with a large cylindrical housing, octagonal base, hazard-striped band, and visible electronic controls and wiring.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cylindrical housing, hazard-striped band, control panel, and wiring clearly visible above the octagonal base.
description_view_1: Rear view highlights the solid support column and the back of the octagonal base, with minimal visible controls or wiring.
description_view_2: Side view reveals a glowing blue patterned panel beneath the housing, along with the support structure and some wiring.
description_view_3: Opposite side view exposes more of the internal components and wiring, with the base and support column visible.
